microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they likely want food warm without overdoing heat. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue makes hotter food and fewer reheats more desirable. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE social context does not outweigh the stable preference for normally warm food. IF eating while working on a laptop or watching TV in front AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE efficiency dominates and hotter food reduces desire for another heating cycle. IF any context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended cold. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND brownie or warm dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE a light warm-up is preferred over making dessert too hot. IF any context AND physically energetic AND meal already served warm or has delicate texture THEN 1 min BECAUSE they tolerate slightly cooler food to preserve texture.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food without a front-facing activity THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE efficiency matters more than view management. IF eating while watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE preserving the front view is important. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility still outweighs speed in this user's tie-break policy for sustained front tasks. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep that partner visible. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep that partner visible. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion matching partner side BECAUSE fatigue does not override the desire to maintain social visibility. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE when no one and nothing important is being viewed, speed wins.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE limited trunk and neck control make comfort and predictability important. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-transfer food or drink THEN medium BECAUSE efficiency can increase slightly without sacrificing too much comfort. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases sensitivity to motion near the face. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE socially comfortable motion is preferred over speed. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE stable, less startling motion reduces distraction. IF any context AND physically energetic AND in a hurry AND simple food without dipping THEN medium BECAUSE this user accepts modest speed increases when both energy and urgency are high. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND messy foods or foods with sauce THEN slow BECAUSE careful motion reduces spill risk.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods with elongated or large pieces of meat or fruit THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites better match slow mouth opening. IF any context AND neutral AND soft foods that are hard to secure on the fork THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE acquisition success matters when bite stability is poor. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND difficult slippery foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed acquisitions is prioritized in social settings. IF eating alone AND physically fatigued AND large firm foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE easier oral acceptance outweighs acquisition efficiency. IF any context AND in a hurry AND easy stab-able foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the stable oral comfort preference remains dominant. IF any context AND in a hurry AND repeated acquisition failures are likely for the current food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE task completion overrides the usual narrow-bite preference. IF any context AND desserts like brownies or compact cubes THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE those foods are usually acquired more reliably that way.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND ordinary foods THEN yes BECAUSE they can use the assistive web interface and prefer reliable transfers over maximum speed. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-acquire foods THEN no BECAUSE efficiency can outweigh confirmation when failure risk is low. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE avoiding visibly failed empty-fork transfers is important. IF eating while working or watching TV in front AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE extra confirmation interrupts the primary activity more than occasional acquisition errors. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE fatigue triggers the user's tie-break policy of minimizing interaction effort. IF any context AND difficult foods like slippery meats, saucy items, or irregular fruit AND neutral THEN yes BECAUSE acquisition uncertainty is high. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND difficult foods THEN yes BECAUSE reliability overrides fatigue when the risk of failed transfer is especially high.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE they cannot reliably lean forward. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical limitations make outside-mouth transfer impractical despite social context. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it reduces the need for precise self-movement. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE fatigue further reduces ability to approach the utensil. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most direct and physically compatible method. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE extra time does not change the user's trunk-control limitation. IF any context AND messy or saucy foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE bringing the utensil fully to the mouth reduces reach-related spillage.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is not physically suitable. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE social context does not change the transfer mode. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still needs direct delivery. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue reinforces inside-mouth use. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the efficient physically compatible mode remains inside-mouth. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE more time does not create leaning ability. IF any context AND hypothetical outside-mouth transfer is forced by system constraints AND any affect AND any food THEN near BECAUSE if outside-mouth must occur, the robot should stop as close as possible to minimize required user movement.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues support slow response without requiring extra effort. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE a quieter cue is less disruptive to conversation while still visible. IF eating while watching TV in front AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE speech may compete with audio from the TV. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE divided attention benefits from redundant notification. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue increases the value of a clear multimodal prompt. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE the user prefers a brief low-overhead cue when moving quickly through the meal. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE comfort and clarity outweigh brevity.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE button use is not reliable and explicit but low-effort control is preferred over full automation. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE open-mouth detection may be confused with talking and button use is unavailable. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND feeding any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue strengthens the low-effort choice already favored in social settings. IF eating while watching TV in front AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE talking-related false triggers are less likely than in conversation. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE they still want some control without needing hands. IF any context AND in a hurry AND feeding easy foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficiency overrides explicit initiation when risk is acceptable. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND feeding any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE when not rushed they prefer to intentionally signal readiness.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE it provides hands-free control and is feasible despite being somewhat slow. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE conversation can interfere with open-mouth readiness detection. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE minimizing effort remains the tie-break winner. IF eating while watching TV in front AND neutral AND drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE there is less conversational ambiguity than in social meals. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE they want to preserve some timing control while occupied. IF any context AND in a hurry AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE sipping transfers can proceed efficiently without manual input. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user prefers intentional initiation when pace pressure is low.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE wiping is ancillary and the user prefers not to add effort for it. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE open-mouth signaling is awkward and button use is infeasible. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE they prefer the robot to handle wiping with minimal interruption. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue strongly favors maximum automation for non-feeding tasks. IF any context AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it is the fastest compatible option. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND mouth wiping is needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE extra time does not create a better low-effort signal. IF any context AND wiping is frequent due to messy saucy foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE repeated readiness signaling would be burdensome.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any transfer task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues help them know exactly when to bite, sip, or wipe. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any transfer task THEN LED BECAUSE a quieter completion cue is less socially intrusive. IF eating while watching TV in front AND neutral AND any transfer task THEN LED BECAUSE speech may mask or compete with media audio. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any transfer task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE divided attention makes redundancy useful at the critical completion moment. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any transfer task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue increases the need for clear timing support. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any transfer task THEN LED BECAUSE a concise cue reduces delay. IF any context AND messy foods or hot drinks AND neutral THEN speech + LED BECAUSE precise awareness of transfer completion timing reduces spills and discomfort.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE it is reliable and avoids impossible button use. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE the fork force signal is reliable and socially unobtrusive. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE it minimizes interruption and does not rely on user gestures. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE fatigue makes manual signaling even less desirable. IF any context AND in a hurry AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE it is both fast and dependable. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE extra time does not make button use more feasible. IF any context AND difficult chewy foods THEN perception BECAUSE it can adapt to variable bite timing better than a fixed timeout.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE head-gesture perception is unreliable for this user and button use is not feasible. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it avoids awkward head gestures during conversation. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it minimizes task interruption. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue further reduces ability to provide explicit completion signals. IF any context AND in a hurry AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it is the fastest compatible option. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE there is still no reliable manual or gestural alternative. IF any context AND hot drinks or cautious sipping conditions THEN autocontinue BECAUSE a conservative timeout is safer than relying on unreliable gestures.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE head gestures are unreliable and button use is not available. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it avoids socially awkward explicit completion gestures. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it keeps the interruption brief. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE low-effort automation is strongly preferred. IF any context AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it speeds through a secondary task. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE no better compatible completion signal exists. IF any context AND messy saucy foods requiring repeated wipes THEN autocontinue BECAUSE repeated explicit completion signaling would be burdensome.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE they prioritize shorter meal duration when occlusion is not important. IF eating while watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the front view matters between bites. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the robot should clear the workspace view. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE retracting reduces prolonged left-side visual obstruction. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE retracting reduces prolonged right-side obstruction. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE fatigue shifts the tie-break toward minimizing total meal time and waiting. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN no BECAUSE efficiency dominates. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food in a view-sensitive setting THEN yes BECAUSE with no time pressure they prefer a less obstructive robot posture.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND no sauce or no dippable food THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is irrelevant. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND dippable foods with a liked sauce THEN more BECAUSE in private they enjoy flavor and are less concerned about mess. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND dippable foods THEN less BECAUSE they want to reduce messiness in front of others. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND dippable foods THEN less BECAUSE spills near electronics are undesirable. IF eating while watching TV in front AND relaxed with ample time AND dippable foods THEN more BECAUSE leisure context supports a more indulgent preference. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND dippable foods THEN less BECAUSE cleaner bites are easier to manage when tired. IF any context AND in a hurry AND dippable foods THEN do not dip BECAUSE skipping dipping reduces time and mess. IF any context AND messy sauces with high drip risk THEN less BECAUSE the user prefers flavor only when it does not substantially increase transfer mess.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE they want enough time to respond with slow mouth opening without making the meal stall excessively. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN 10 sec BECAUSE efficiency dominates when they want a faster meal. IF eating in a social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE conversation may delay responses but extremely long waits would feel awkward. IF eating while watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE divided attention calls for moderate patience. IF any context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE fatigue can slow responses substantially and the user prefers the system not to rush them. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE they want maximal opportunity to intervene or compose themselves. IF any context AND in a hurry AND simple repetitive drinking or wiping tasks THEN 10 sec BECAUSE those tasks benefit most from fast automation. IF any context AND difficult foods or socially distracting settings AND physically fatigued THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the tie-break policy favors giving extra time when both distraction and reduced energy are present.
